event_tagline: Human out of the loop in high-risk financial workflows

main_tracks.name: Money Movement
main_tracks.summary: Agents that actually move money. A wrong action means real money is gone.
main_tracks.description: Workflows where your agent moves money — paying invoices, running payroll, sending refunds, transferring between accounts. The risk is direct: get it wrong and the money is gone. The human-in-the-loop question: how big is too big to act alone, what counts as suspicious, and who can the agent trust?
examples: Agent that auto-pays small invoices and escalates the rest
Payroll runner that pauses when something looks off
Refund bot that handles routine cases and asks before the rest

main_tracks.name: Financial Intelligence
main_tracks.summary: Agents that read, interpret, and explain. A wrong answer means a wrong decision downstream.
main_tracks.description: Agents that read and interpret money — categorising transactions, spotting fraud, explaining bank lines, answering "what is this charge for?". The risk is indirect: a wrong answer becomes a wrong decision downstream. The human-in-the-loop question: how confident is the agent really, and which edge cases deserve a human eye?
examples: Agent that categorises transactions and flags the ones it isn't sure about
Fraud detector that asks a human when the signal is mixed
Bot that explains every line on a bank statement

criteria.name: Concrete Workflow Value
criteria.description: Does it replace or compress a real finance workflow a human does today?

criteria.name: Track Fit
criteria.description: How purely does the submission embody its chosen track (money movement vs financial intelligence)?

criteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Decision
criteria.description: Does the system know when a human should be in the loop vs not? Thresholds, confidence gates, escalation paths.

criteria.name: Technical Execution
criteria.description: Architecture quality, tool design, latency, integrations that actually work.

criteria.name: Demo Clarity
criteria.description: Can the judge, in 90 seconds, see exactly what this agent does and why it matters?

judge_bonus_bucket.name: Judge Bonus Bucket
judge_bonus_bucket.description: Four sponsor-aligned buckets (8 + 8 + 7 + 7 points). Judges score each bucket independently — 30 bonus total.

side_quests.name: Best use of Cursor
side_quests.blurb: How effectively the build used Cursor — editor, agents, and workflow — end to end.

side_quests.name: Best use of Specter
side_quests.blurb: Standout use of Specter's API, MCP, or data for market intelligence in the product.

side_quests.name: Best use of Financial Practices
side_quests.blurb: Depth on real finance rails, compliance, or domain fit — not a slide deck.

side_quests.name: Best use of LLM models
side_quests.blurb: Smart or effective use of models — APIs, routing, evals, or multi-model design.

prizes.name: 1st place
prizes.description: Top-scoring submission across both tracks. Cash and half a year on Cursor's top tier.

prizes.name: 3rd place
prizes.description: Third highest score across both tracks.
